park_comments: Fee site.  Elevation 6100ft.  Campsites can be cold and windy year round.  The tallest mountains in Idaho are to your east and a historic mining district to the west.  Many POTA Parks nearby.  Can be activated year round but Jul - Sep are best.

name: Chilly Slough Wetland Conservation Area
park_comments: Interpretive site 17 miles north of Mackay on US 95, mile marker 126.  Vehicle mounted antennas only.  Can be cold and windy.  Take binoculars for bird watching.  Moose, elk, antelope, and deer may also be seen.  No facilities.

name: Cape Sebastian Corridor
park_comments: Official site https://stateparks.oregon.gov/index.cfm?do=park.profile&parkId=52  Use the boundaries using the Oregon Geohub site linked above.

name: Salmon Falls Reservoir
park_comments: 4986ft elevation.  Includes Salmon Falls Creek south to the Nevada line.  Activate within 100ft of the reservoir high water mark.  Avoid roads when wet.  Exercise caution around waterfront cliff bands.  Predators and rattlesnakes

name: Thorn Creek Reservoir
park_comments: Primitive camping.  Rough rutted road not suitable for larger RV trailers.  Best access June thru October.  Activate with 100ft of high water mark.  Elevation 5500ft.  Predators, ticks, and rattlesnakes.  4wd recommended east of dam.

name: Magic Reservoir
park_comments: Activate within 100ft of high water mark.  Primitive camping.  Magic Reservoir Airstrip is state property, not a two'fer.  Best access May thru Oct.  Elevation 4800ft. Caution camping on waters edge, water levels change daily.  Predators and rattlesnakes.

name: GrasmereReservoir
park_comments: Remote.  Elevation 5200ft.  Rough rocky access road.  Includes both reservoirs. Primitive camping.  Best access May thru October.  Predators and rattlesnakes.

name: Fish Creek Reservoir
park_comments: Activate within 100ft of high water mark.  Semi developed camping.  Water levels vary by year.  Best access Jun thru Oct.  Predators and rattlesnakes.

name: Silver Creek North and South
park_comments: Limited overnight camping.  Elevation 4800ft.   World class fly fishing.  Predators and rattlesnakes.

name: Weiser Dunes Recreation Site
park_comments: Primitive camping. Nearby active railroad.  Can be very hot in warmer months.  Predators and rattlesnakes.

name: Brownlee/Oxbow Reservoir
park_comments: Includes all reservoir waters upstream from both the Brownlee and Oxbow Dams.  Must activate within 100ft of high water mark.  Watch for cliff band rockfall.  Predators and snakes.  Limited cell coverage
